aids.title: JDBC Connection Steps - LGESC
aids.content: **L**oad driver (Class.forName), **G**et connection (DriverManager.getConnection), **E**xecute query (Statement), **S**can results (ResultSet), **C**lose resources. LGESC - 'Let's Get Every SQL Connection.'

aids.title: JDBC Driver Types 1-2-3-4
aids.content: Type 1: Bridge (ODBC), Type 2: Native (partial Java), Type 3: Network (middleware), Type 4: Thin (pure Java, direct). Type 4 is the best - 'Four is the Floor' (foundation).

aids.title: JDBC URL Format
aids.content: jdbc:subprotocol:subname. Always starts with 'jdbc:'. Examples: jdbc:mysql://host:3306/db, jdbc:oracle:thin:@host:1521:db, jdbc:postgresql://host:5432/db

aids.title: Connection Pooling Analogy
aids.content: Connection pooling is like a car rental service. Instead of building a new car for each customer (expensive), you maintain a fleet and assign available cars. When done, the car returns to the pool for the next customer.
